text:
Olympics 2024star George Mills apparently shouted, “you f***ing clown” as he squared up to French rival Hugo Hay in the aftermath of the first 5,000 metre heat.Mills took a tumble on the home straightas a whole host of athletes collided.

The 5,000m race was run at a lethargic pace as athletes attempted to book their place in the semi-finals during a tactical battle.

But Mills hit into other athletes after he was given a slight push by Hay during the closing stages.

Mills, 25, marched over to his French rival after walking across the line and squared up to the 27-year-old before giving him a shove in the chest. And expert lipreader Jeremy Freeman told theDaily Mailthat as Mills was approaching Hay and pointing his finger at the athlete, he exclaimed: “You f***ing clown”.

Hay appeared to protest his innocence, with Mills doubling down with his criticism as he added: “You f***ing did”. The Briton then twice exclaimed: “Yes you did.”

Hay replied by saying: “No it was not me”. And the Frenchman appeared to grow frustrated with the exchange as Mills stormed away as he added: “Ah come on”.

Read more:Olympic athlete arrested 'on suspicion of trying to buy cocaine in Paris'



Discovery+is the new home of the Olympics. The streamer is set to broadcast 3,800 hours of sport live from Paris.

Sports fans can getDiscovery+ for £3.99a month until the end of 2024 via Amazon Prime's video channels. This also comes with anAmazon Prime 30-day free trial.

After the race, Mills spoke to the BBC about the incident and explained: “I think it's pretty clear. I got stepped out on as I was about to kick on the home straight and boom, the French lad took me down.

“From my perspective that was the perfect qualifier for me. Going through the first 2,000 in six minutes. I was like, 'Alright - nobody in this field can run away from me at this pace'.

“So I was just sitting, waiting and biding my time, gonna kick off down the home straight and bang hit the deck. What can you do?”

Don't miss:Team GB star went deaf mid-race after pushing body to limit for Olympic medal

Don't miss:Olympics cameraman walks into middle of 5,000m race leaving athletes fuming

Don't miss:Team GB star goes back to work this week after missing out on medal in Paris

Meanwhile, Hay stated in his post-race interview: “For me, that was nothing. Like I said to George, I will watch the images and if I'm the guy that did it, I will apologise. For me, I didn't do anything. I just hope that I can go in the final.”

The heat was ultimately won by Narve Gilje Nordas from Norway, who raced clear in the aftermath of the tumble. Ethiopia’s Hagos Gebrhiwet clinched second, with Belgium star John Heymans finishing in third.

Mills was eventually reinstated by the race referee and will be allowed to compete in the 5,000m semi-finals. Dominic Lokinyomo Lobalu, Mike Foppen and Thierry Ndikumwenayo were also handed places in the next round.

title: George Mills' three-word insult to Olympic rival after being tripped in 5,000m race | Other | Sport | Express.co.uk
author: Tom Parsons
description: Olympics star George Mills took a tumble in the first 5,000 metre heat at the Paris Games.
